commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0f7ac960aa49ce887a2c8e33eeb64b1241c75056)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Claude live can_use_tool allow responses to return updatedInput and updates focused tests for that payload shape.

PR surface: Source +15, Tests +41. Total +56 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. The linked issue gives concrete OpenClaw 2026.6.8 plus Claude Code 2.1.156 steps, and current main still emits the old allow payload without updatedInput; I did not run a live Claude CLI approval flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blocker combines external Claude Code protocol compatibility, incomplete contributor proof, and multiple open sibling candidates.

target.title: fix(cli-runner): return updatedInput for allowed Claude can_use_tool responses
target.body:
### What Problem This Solves

Claude Code 2.1.156 rejects the allow response OpenClaw sends for `can_use_tool` control requests because it still uses the deprecated `{ behavior: "allow" }` shape. Every tool call that goes through the permission/approval flow fails with a ZodError before the tool runs (see #95171).

### Why This Change Was Made

The new Claude Code `PermissionResult` schema expects the allow branch to be `{ updatedInput: <input> }`. This change returns the original tool input back to Claude for allowed native tool calls, while keeping the existing deny branch unchanged.

### User Impact

Users on Claude Code >= 2.1.156 can now use tools that hit the exec approval flow (e.g. `Bash`, `WebFetch`, `Grep` outside cwd) without getting a ZodError.

### Evidence

Added `src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.can-use-tool.test.ts`.

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.can-use-tool.test.ts
```

Result:

```
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668001525/PR解决/openclaw

 ✓ |unit-fast| src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.can-use-tool.test.ts (2 tests) 6ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed (2)
   Start at  11:09:38
   Duration  11.98s (transform 6.88s, setup 0ms, import 11.68s, tests 6ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 21.82s
```

### Real behavior proof

Protocol-level verification against the installed Claude Code 2.1.76 binary, using the actual PR code path:

```
=== Claude Code version ===
2.1.76 (Claude Code)

=== OpenClaw can_use_tool allow response (real PR code) ===
{
  "type": "control_response",
  "response": {
    "subtype": "success",
    "request_id": "req-allow-1",
    "response": {
      "updatedInput": {
        "command": "date"
      }
    }
  }
}

=== OpenClaw can_use_tool deny response (real PR code) ===
{
  "type": "control_response",
  "response": {
    "subtype": "success",
    "request_id": "req-deny-1",
    "response": {
      "behavior": "deny",
      "decisionClassification": "user_reject",
      "message": "OpenClaw exec policy denied Claude native tool use."
    }
  }
}

✓ Allow response contains updatedInput with original tool input

=== Claude Code 2.1.x initialize handshake ===
Claude Code initialize succeeded
Available models: Default (recommended), Sonnet (1M context), Opus, Opus (1M context), Haiku ...

=== Proof summary ===
- Claude Code binary: 2.1.76 (Claude Code)
- OpenClaw allow response shape: { updatedInput: <tool input> }
- Claude Code 2.1.x initialize control_response handshake: OK
```

This confirms the actual OpenClaw code path now emits `{ updatedInput: <tool input> }` for allowed `can_use_tool` requests, matching the Claude Code 2.1.x `PermissionResult` allow branch. A complete end-to-end recording of an approved live tool call reaching execution (e.g. `Bash("date")`) requires Anthropic API access, which is not present in this environment.

Fixes #95171
target.author: moguangyu5-design

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
